pattern: Range-with-step multiplicative updates over a modulus are folded into one XOR, where the larger bounds reward grouping work by step size rather than replaying every query naively.
hints: Every query updates an arithmetic progression of indices li, li+ki, li+2ki, ... within [li, ri] - the touched positions depend on both the step and the start.
Apply each query's multiplier in order, reducing modulo 1e9+7 after each multiplication so values stay bounded.
Once all queries are applied, XOR the entire array together for the final answer.

edge_inputs.description: one query covering every index with step one
step larger than range so only the start index is touched
multiplier of one leaves values unchanged
product crosses the modulus on a single index
repeated multipliers on the same index compound

stress.description: moderate array and many random queries with varied steps - many small ranges plus some full sweeps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 10**9 + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    q = n
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        l = rng.randrange(0, n)
        r = rng.randrange(l, n)
        k = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
        v = rng.randrange(1, 10**5 + 1)
        queries.append([l, r, k, v])
    return [nums, queries]
stress.note: values stay positive so XOR is over non-negative integers below the modulus
